questions.prompt: In a balanced three-phase system, the phase voltages are separated by:
questions.explanation: Three-phase: phases A, B, C displaced by 120° (360°/3).

questions.prompt: In a balanced Y (wye) system, the line voltage V_L is related to phase voltage V_P by:
questions.explanation: Y-connected: V_L = √3 × V_P ≈ 1.732 V_P. E.g., 120 V phase gives 208 V line.

questions.prompt: In a balanced delta (Δ) system, the line current I_L is related to phase current I_P by:
questions.explanation: Delta: I_L = √3 × I_P. (Opposite of the wye voltage relationship.)

questions.prompt: The total real power in a balanced three-phase system is:
choices: A. P = 3 V_P I_P cos θ
B. P = √3 V_L I_L cos θ
C. P = 3 V_L I_L cos θ
questions.explanation: P = 3V_P I_P cos θ = √3 V_L I_L cos θ. Both are equivalent (using Y or Δ relationships).

questions.prompt: A balanced three-phase Y load has V_L = 208 V (line-to-line) and each phase impedance Z = 10 Ω (resistive). What is the total real power in watts?
In a balanced Y-connected system, the neutral current I_N is:
questions.explanation: In a balanced Y system, the three phase currents sum to zero → neutral current = 0.

questions.prompt: The phase sequence ABC (positive sequence) means:
choices: A. V_A leads V_B by 120°, V_B leads V_C by 120°
C. All three voltages are in phase
questions.explanation: Positive (ABC) sequence: V_A → V_B → V_C, each lagging the previous by 120°. V_A leads V_B by 120°.

questions.prompt: For a Δ-connected load with equal impedances Z_Δ, the equivalent Y-connected impedance Z_Y is:
questions.explanation: Δ-to-Y conversion: Z_Y = Z_Δ / 3. (Y arm is one-third of delta arm for balanced systems.)

questions.prompt: The two-wattmeter method measures total three-phase power by:
questions.explanation: Two-wattmeter method: P_total = W₁ + W₂. The reactive power Q = √3(W₁ − W₂).

questions.prompt: In a balanced three-phase system, apparent power S is related to real P and reactive Q by:
questions.explanation: S = √(P² + Q²) in VA; same relationship as single-phase complex power.

questions.prompt: Line voltage in a 480 V (line-to-line) three-phase system equals _____ V phase-to-neutral.
questions.explanation: V_P = V_L / √3 = 480 / 1.732 ≈ 277 V. (North American 480 V systems: 277 V phase-to-neutral.)

questions.prompt: An unbalanced three-phase load _____ have a zero neutral current.
questions.explanation: Unbalanced loads generally have nonzero neutral current because the phasor sum of three unequal phase currents ≠ 0.

questions.prompt: Which are advantages of three-phase power over single-phase? (Select ALL that apply.)
choices: A. Constant instantaneous power delivery (no pulsation for balanced loads)
B. More efficient use of conductors for same power
C. Self-starting rotating magnetic field for motors
D. Always lower voltage per phase
questions.explanation: Three-phase provides constant power, better copper utilization, and a rotating field for induction motors. Voltage relationship depends on connection type, not always lower.

questions.prompt: Total reactive power Q in a balanced three-phase system is:
choices: A. Q = 3 V_P I_P sin θ = √3 V_L I_L sin θ
B. Q = V_L I_L sin θ
C. Q = 3 V_L I_L sin θ
D. Q = √3 V_P I_P sin θ
questions.explanation: Q = 3V_P I_P sin θ = √3 V_L I_L sin θ (VAR). Same structure as real power formula.

questions.prompt: In a delta (Δ)-connected system, the line-to-line voltage equals:
questions.explanation: In delta connection, each phase element is connected directly across a line pair: V_line = V_phase.

questions.prompt: A balanced 3-phase Y load on a 120 V (phase-to-neutral) system with Z = 6 + j8 Ω per phase. What is the total apparent power S in VA? (Hint: I_P = V_P/|Z|)
The per-unit system in power systems normalizes quantities by:
choices: B. Dividing actual values by chosen base quantities
questions.explanation: Per-unit: Z_pu = Z_actual / Z_base. Simplifies calculations in transformer and multi-voltage systems.

questions.prompt: Symmetrical components break an unbalanced three-phase system into:
choices: A. Positive, negative, and zero sequence components
B. Real, imaginary, and zero components
C. Line, phase, and neutral components
D. Fundamental, second, and third harmonic
questions.explanation: Fortescue's symmetrical components: any unbalanced 3-phase set = positive + negative + zero sequence balanced sets.

questions.prompt: A three-phase transformer with delta primary and wye secondary causes a ___° phase shift between primary and secondary line voltages.
questions.explanation: Delta-wye (Δ-Y) transformers produce a 30° phase shift (by IEEE convention, wye lags delta by 30°).

questions.prompt: Power factor in a balanced three-phase circuit is:
choices: A. PF = cos(angle between line voltage and line current)
B. PF = cos(θ_Z) where θ_Z is the phase impedance angle
questions.explanation: All three definitions are equivalent: the impedance angle = voltage-current angle; PF = cos θ = P/S.

questions.prompt: In a wye-connected generator with phase sequence A-B-C and V_A = 100∠0°, V_B = ?
questions.explanation: ABC sequence (positive): V_B lags V_A by 120°: V_B = 100∠−120°.

questions.prompt: For a balanced three-phase load, adding a neutral conductor:
choices: A. Allows single-phase loads but does not change balanced three-phase operation
D. Is required for all three-phase systems
questions.explanation: The neutral carries zero current under balanced conditions; it enables single-phase loads and protects against voltage unbalance.

questions.prompt: In 120/208 V three-phase Y system, '208 V' refers to:
questions.explanation: 208 V = √3 × 120 V ≈ 208 V is the line-to-line voltage; 120 V is the phase-to-neutral voltage.

questions.prompt: A three-phase induction motor runs most efficiently at:
questions.explanation: Induction motors are designed for rated load efficiency; efficiency drops at no load (mostly magnetizing losses) and light load.

questions.prompt: Line voltage in a 480 V (L-L) three-phase system equals approximately how many volts phase-to-neutral?
